guitars.title: Ornetts GM5RC
guitars.subtitle: Honey Burst Sunburst · Near Mint · High End Guitars, Nagano
guitars.eyebrow: 2006 · Made in Japan · Nagano · High End Guitars
guitars.meta_title: 2006 Ornetts GM5RC Honey Burst — Rare Nagano Japan Guitar | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Near mint 2006 Ornetts GM5RC in Honey Burst. Made in Nagano Japan by High End Guitars. Figured maple top, heel-access truss rod, hardshell case. CA$2,599.
guitars.description: An extraordinarily rare instrument — Ornetts guitars were made by High End Guitars in Nagano, Japan for only a few short years. This example appears unplayed, as if it spent its entire life in the case. The figured maple top is stunning, the neck is perfect, and the unique heel-access truss rod makes setup effortless. You won't find many of these anywhere — including Japan.

guitars.title: Fender Japan ST62
guitars.subtitle: Surf Green (SGB) · Mint / Unplayed · 1962 Stratocaster Reissue
guitars.eyebrow: 2010 · Made in Japan · Tokai Plant · Fender Japan
guitars.meta_title: 2010 Fender Japan ST62 Surf Green — Unplayed MIJ Stratocaster | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Unplayed 2010 Fender Japan ST62 Surf Green (SGB). Original plastic still on pickguard. Discontinued colourway. Serial L-044575. Fender gig bag. CA$1,899.
guitars.description: A genuine time capsule. This 2010 Fender Japan ST62 in the coveted Surf Green (SGB) colorway has never been played — original protective plastic film is still on the pickguard and all three pickup covers. Surf Green was quietly discontinued from the ST62 lineup in 2015 when Fender Japan retired the entire reissue series, making surviving examples — let alone unplayed ones — exceptionally rare. Serial L-044575 confirms the 2010 build date.

guitars.title: Navigator CTM 320
guitars.subtitle: Jet Black · Les Paul Custom · All Original · ESP Custom Shop
guitars.eyebrow: 2004 · Made in Japan · ESP Custom Shop · Navigator
guitars.meta_title: 2004 Navigator CTM 320 Jet Black — ESP Custom Shop Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2004 Navigator CTM 320 Jet Black Les Paul Custom. ESP Custom Shop Japan. Gold Gotoh hardware, abalone block inlays, serial K0403501. 8.8 lbs. CA$4,499.
guitars.description: Jet black with gold binding, abalone block inlays, and Gold Gotoh tuners. The Navigator CTM 320 is exactly what the ESP Custom Shop built for Japan's most serious domestic players — instruments that were never meant to leave the country. This 2004 example has been professionally set up and is all original. At 8.8 lbs it has the weight and resonance of a guitar that means business. Navigator ceased production in July 2025, making every example more significant.

guitars.title: Navigator N-335
guitars.subtitle: Natural Finish · Semi-Hollow · All Original · Golden Era
guitars.eyebrow: 1970s–80s · Made in Japan · ESP Custom Shop · Navigator
guitars.meta_title: Navigator N-335 Natural Semi-Hollow 1970s — ESP Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Vintage Navigator N-335 semi-hollow Natural finish. All original hardware. 1970s–80s ESP Custom Shop Japan domestic guitar. 8.5 lbs. CA$2,999.
guitars.description: A stunning example of Navigator's domestic Japan semi-hollow craftsmanship from the golden era. Natural finish over a beautifully grained top, all original hardware and electronics intact. These early Navigator 335-style instruments were built by ESP's finest luthiers exclusively for the Japanese domestic market — the kind of guitar that simply doesn't leave Japan very often. Navigator ceased production in July 2025.

guitars.title: Navigator N-335
guitars.subtitle: Violin Burst · Semi-Hollow · All Original · Golden Era
guitars.eyebrow: 1970s–80s · Made in Japan · ESP Custom Shop · Navigator
guitars.meta_title: Navigator N-335 Violin Burst Semi-Hollow 1970s — ESP Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Vintage Navigator N-335 Violin Burst semi-hollow. All original. 1970s–80s ESP Custom Shop Japan. Deep amber gradient finish. Discontinued 2025. CA$2,499.
guitars.description: That deep amber-to-mahogany gradient — the colour of a fine cello — gives this semi-hollow a warmth you can see before you even plug it in. Built in Japan during ESP's golden domestic era, every detail on this guitar tells the same story: uncompromising craftsmanship, premium materials, and a tone that only decades of aging can produce. All original hardware and electronics, exactly as it left the workshop. Navigator stopped production in July 2025.

guitars.title: Ibanez AR500
guitars.subtitle: Cherry Sunburst · Artist Series · 1 of 162 Made · FujiGen
guitars.eyebrow: 1982 · Made in Japan · FujiGen · Ibanez Artist Series
guitars.meta_title: 1982 Ibanez AR500 Cherry Sunburst — 1 of 162 Made · FujiGen Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 1982 Ibanez AR500 Cherry Sunburst. Only 162 produced. FujiGen Japan. Professionally restored binding, full setup by R Guitars. Original gig bag. CA$4,499.
guitars.description: Only 162 of these were built in 1982 — and this is one of them. The AR500 was Ibanez's flagship Artist Series model at a time when FujiGen was producing some of the finest guitars on the planet. This example has received the full treatment from R Guitars: binding professionally restored, full setup, and every detail brought back to its original glory. All original hardware and electronics, paired with that iconic Cherry Sunburst that seems to glow from within. If you know what an AR500 is, you already know why this price won't last.

guitars.title: Fender Telecaster CIJ
guitars.subtitle: The B-Bender Mystery · Case Queen · One-Off Custom Build
guitars.eyebrow: 2004–05 · Crafted in Japan · Fender · Mystery Build
guitars.meta_title: 2004 Fender Telecaster CIJ B-Bender Mystery — Case Queen Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Mystery 2004–05 Fender CIJ Telecaster in original B-Bender case with no B-Bender. Custom pickguard, almost unplayed case queen. Made in Japan. CA$1,599.
guitars.description: Some guitars come with a story. This one comes with a mystery. It arrived in an original Fender B-Bender Telecaster case, complete with B-Bender case candy and documentation — but the guitar itself has no B-Bender. What it does have is a custom-made pickguard, a matching strap, and all the hallmarks of a one-off special build that somehow ended up with the wrong paperwork. Almost entirely unplayed — a true case queen. Whether Fender built it as a custom, swapped it at the factory, or something stranger happened, nobody knows. That's part of what you're buying.

guitars.title: History SH-LSV/FM Heritage Wood
guitars.subtitle: Cherry Sunburst · Flame Maple · Ancient Great Lakes Tonewood
guitars.eyebrow: 2012 · Made in Japan · FujiGen · Heritage Wood Series
guitars.meta_title: 2012 History SH-LSV/FM Heritage Wood Cherry Sunburst — FujiGen Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2012 History SH-LSV/FM Heritage Wood series. Ancient Great Lakes tonewood. Flame maple top, cherry sunburst. FujiGen Japan exclusive. Hard case. CA$1,899.
guitars.description: History guitars occupy a category of their own — built by FujiGen exclusively for a single Japanese retailer, never exported, never mass-marketed. The Heritage Wood series goes even further: the tonewoods are sourced from ancient logs recovered from the Great Lakes, some of them hundreds of years old, dried and aged far beyond what any conventional supplier can offer. The result is a resonance and warmth that simply cannot be replicated with freshly milled wood. This 2012 SH-LSV/FM wears a deep cherry sunburst over a stunning flame maple top and arrives with its genuine History hard case.

guitars.title: History Les Paul
guitars.subtitle: Honeyburst · Player's Grade · FujiGen Japan Exclusive
guitars.eyebrow: 2005 · Made in Japan · FujiGen · History Guitars
guitars.meta_title: 2005 History Les Paul Honeyburst Player's Grade — FujiGen Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2005 History Les Paul Honeyburst. Player's grade. FujiGen Japan exclusive. 7.6 lbs, 43mm nut, 53mm 12th fret. Built for one Japanese retailer. CA$1,499.
guitars.description: Not every great guitar lives in a display case. This 2005 History Les Paul has been played — and you can tell, because guitars that get played this much tend to sound like this. The dings and dents are a roadmap of a life well-lived, and the wood underneath doesn't lie: the grain is exceptional, and the sustain is the kind that makes you forget what song you were playing. At 7.6 lbs and 43mm nut it sits in that sweet spot — light enough to wear all night, fast enough to play all night. History guitars are built by FujiGen for a single Japanese retailer and never exported.

guitars.title: Navigator N-LP 480LTD
guitars.subtitle: Lemonburst · True '59-Spec · Production Ended July 2025
guitars.eyebrow: 2009 · Made in Japan · ESP Custom Shop · Navigator
guitars.meta_title: 2009 Navigator N-LP 480LTD Lemonburst — Discontinued 2025 | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2009 Navigator N-LP 480LTD Lemonburst. ESP Custom Shop Japan. Production ended July 2025. True '59-spec Les Paul, 8.8 lbs. Navigator hard case. CA$3,999.
guitars.description: Navigator stopped production in July 2025. That's not a sales pitch — it's a fact that changes the math on every Navigator still in circulation. What was already one of Japan's most coveted domestic guitar lines is now a closed chapter, and the market is only beginning to catch up to what that means. This 2009 N-LP 480LTD is the real thing: a true '59-spec Les Paul built by ESP's finest luthiers, in a lemonburst that glows like amber held up to the light. At 8.8 lbs it has the weight and density that serious players associate with sustain and authority. Comes in its genuine Navigator hard case.

guitars.title: Moon Custom Stratocaster
guitars.subtitle: Violin Burst · True Custom Relic · Brass Nut · Tokyo
guitars.eyebrow: Made in Japan · Moon Custom Guitars · Tokyo
guitars.meta_title: Moon Custom Stratocaster Violin Burst Relic — Tokyo Boutique MIJ | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Moon Custom Guitars Tokyo Stratocaster in Violin Burst. True custom relic, brass nut. 7.1 lbs. Boutique Japanese guitar, session player favourite. CA$1,399.
guitars.description: Moon Guitars out of Tokyo has been quietly building some of Japan's finest custom instruments for decades — known to insiders, unknown to everyone else, which is exactly how the best things tend to work. This custom Stratocaster arrives in a violin burst that looks like it was pulled from a 1960s jazz club. The relic work is the real thing: not factory-distressed, but a true custom aged finish. At 7.1 lbs it hangs perfectly, and the brass nut adds a warmth and sustain to open strings that synthetic nuts simply can't touch.

guitars.title: Crews Maniac Sound KTR ST60
guitars.subtitle: Sunburst · K&T Vintage Weep Pickups · Tokyo Boutique
guitars.eyebrow: Made in Japan · Crews Maniac Sound · Tokyo
guitars.meta_title: Crews Maniac Sound KTR ST60 Sunburst — K&T Vintage Weep Pickups | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Crews Maniac Sound KTR ST60 Sunburst with K&T Vintage Weep hand-wound pickups. Tokyo boutique builder. One of Japan's finest S-Type guitars. CA$1,999.
guitars.description: There are boutique pickups. Then there are K&T Vintage Weep pickups — and the gap between those two categories is significant. Hand-wound in Japan by Kunimoto & Takahashi, the Vintage Weep set is the most coveted single-coil upgrade on the planet among players who have actually heard them. The word that keeps coming up is weep — a singing, vocal quality in the upper mids that makes a Stratocaster sound like it's expressing something. Pair that with Crews Maniac Sound's obsessive Tokyo craftsmanship and this KTR ST60 in sunburst is something genuinely rare.

guitars.title: Edwards SG
guitars.subtitle: Heritage Cherry · Seymour Duncan P-Rails · Edwards by ESP
guitars.meta_title: 2002 Edwards SG Heritage Cherry — Seymour Duncan P-Rails · ESP Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2002 Edwards SG Heritage Cherry. Seymour Duncan P-Rails — single coil/P-90/humbucker in one. ESP Japan domestic. 7 lbs, ESP gig bag. Excellent condition. CA$999.
guitars.description: Edwards is ESP's mid-tier domestic Japanese line — priced below Navigator, built above almost everything else on the market. This 2002 SG in heritage cherry makes you question every assumption about what a budget SG should be. The real story is inside: Seymour Duncan P-Rails — single coil, P-90, and humbucker all in one pickup per position, via push-pull. You're not choosing between tones, you're choosing between guitars. At 7 lbs it's light enough to wear for hours, excellent shape with very little wear, and comes with its ESP gig bag.

guitars.title: History Black Beauty Dignity Custom
guitars.subtitle: Ebony Black · Les Paul Custom · FujiGen Japan Exclusive
guitars.eyebrow: 2003 · Made in Japan · FujiGen · History Guitars
guitars.meta_title: 2003 History Black Beauty Dignity Custom — FujiGen Japan Exclusive | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2003 History Black Beauty Dignity Custom. FujiGen Japan exclusive, never exported. Ebony black, gold hardware, 10.2 lbs. 43mm nut. Gig bag included. CA$1,499.
guitars.description: The Black Beauty is one of the most iconic guitar silhouettes ever conceived — jet black, triple-bound, gold hardware, the kind of instrument that commands attention before a single note is played. History's Dignity Custom doesn't interpret that template so much as perfect it. Built by FujiGen exclusively for a single Japanese retailer and never exported, this 2003 example has had over two decades to settle into itself. At 10.2 lbs it has the density and sustain that only genuine mahogany construction can deliver. The neck is fast and player-friendly: 43mm at the nut, 52mm at the 12th fret.

guitars.title: Tokai LC60
guitars.subtitle: Wine Red · All Original · 45 Years Old · Case Queen
guitars.eyebrow: 1980 · Made in Japan · Tokai Gakki · Hamamatsu
guitars.meta_title: 1980 Tokai LC60 Wine Red — All Original Case Queen | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 1980 Tokai LC60 Wine Red. All original, 45 years in a case. Tokai Gakki Hamamatsu Japan. 8.8 lbs, 45mm nut. One of the finest Japanese Les Pauls ever made. CA$2,799.
guitars.description: In 1980, Tokai wasn't making guitars inspired by the classics — they were making guitars that beat them. The LC60 is the instrument that put the vintage guitar world on notice: a Japanese Les Paul built with such precision that players who owned both often couldn't tell the difference. Gibson's lawyers certainly noticed. This one is wine red, all original, and has spent 45 years in a case — the hardware, the pickups, the finish, everything exactly as it left the factory in Hamamatsu. At 8.8 lbs it carries the weight and density of a guitar that was built to last a century. It already has.

guitars.title: Killer KG-Wishbone-V
guitars.subtitle: Natural Swamp Ash · EMG 81/89 · Floyd Rose · ESP Japan
guitars.eyebrow: Made in Japan · ESP / Killer Guitars · Rare
guitars.meta_title: Killer KG-Wishbone-V — ESP Japan Boutique · EMG Floyd Rose | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: Killer KG-Wishbone-V by ESP Japan. Natural swamp ash, EMG 81/89, Floyd Rose, ebony board. 8.6 lbs, 44mm nut. Full pro setup. Gig bag. Extremely rare. CA$3,999.
guitars.description: Killer Guitars is one of Japan's most fiercely underground brands — built by ESP's finest craftspeople for a domestic market that simply doesn't export these instruments. The KG-Wishbone-V is the proof: a Flying V reimagined with an ergonomic lower bout carved from 3-piece swamp ash. EMG 81 at the bridge, 89 at the neck — active clarity that cuts through anything, with the 89's coil-split giving you genuine single-coil articulation. Floyd Rose keeps it in tune through everything. Just received a full professional setup at The Guitar Shoppe. This is the rare beast you tell people about.
specs: Full Pro Setup · The Guitar Shoppe

guitars.title: History Les Paul Heritage Wood
guitars.subtitle: Sunburst · Ancient Great Lakes Tonewood · FujiGen Japan
guitars.eyebrow: 2011 · Made in Japan · FujiGen · History Guitars · Heritage Wood
guitars.meta_title: 2011 History Les Paul Heritage Wood Sunburst — Ancient Tonewood Japan | Tsunami Guitars
guitars.meta_desc: 2011 History Les Paul Heritage Wood Sunburst. Ancient Great Lakes tonewood, flame maple top. FujiGen Japan exclusive. 7.6 lbs. Never exported. CA$1,699.
guitars.description: The Heritage Wood stamp on the back of this headstock is a designation most players will walk right past — and serious collectors will stop cold for. The tonewoods in this guitar were sourced from ancient logs recovered from the Great Lakes, some of them hundreds of years old, already dried and aged beyond what any commercial supplier could offer. Wood that old resonates differently. FujiGen built this 2011 Les Paul exclusively for a single Japanese retailer — never exported — and finished it in a sunburst over a stunning flame maple top. At 7.6 lbs it sits in the sweetest weight range for a Les Paul.
